title: Matched MediSave Scheme
text:
Matched MediSave Scheme - SupportGoWhere
Matched MediSave Scheme
Share link
Helps eligible Singapore Citizens to strengthen MediSave adequacy by matching cash top-ups made to their MediSave Accounts (MA).
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Dollar-for-dollar matching grant on cash top-ups to your MA of up to $1,000 per year
Build MA savings to help pay approved medical expenses and insurance premiums, e.g. for MediShield Life
Earn risk-free CPF interest rates of up to 6% per annum on cash top-ups and matching grant
Collapse all sections
About this support

The Government will match every dollar of cash top-ups made to the MA of eligible beneficiaries, up to $1,000 per year, starting from January 2026, for a period of five years

The matching grant will automatically be credited to the MA of the eligible member at the beginning of the following year, i.e. from 2027

Eligible beneficiaries will enjoy interest rates of up to 6% per annum on the cash top-ups and matching grant

Cash top-ups that attract the Matched MediSave Scheme matching grant will not qualify for tax relief

Who is this for?

Singapore Citizen

Between 55 and 70 (inclusive) as of 31 December of the year

Does not own more than 1 property

Annual Value of residence not more than $21,000

Average monthly income not more than $4,000

MA balance less than half the current Basic Healthcare Sum

How to apply?

No application is required

Eligibility is automatically assessed annually at the beginning of the year, starting from January 2026

If you are eligible, CPF Board will notify you via email or post

To receive the matching grant, you need to receive cash top-ups in your MA within the year that you are eligible for Matched MediSave Scheme

Where can I find help?

View more details

CPF hotline (within Singapore): 1800-227-1188 
CPF hotline (from overseas): +65-6227-1188 

Book an appointment to avoid waiting on the line.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch

Note: Please book an appointment before making a visit.

Scheme last updated 12 Feb 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
